guideline_references: ANZCOR Guideline 9.2 (recovery position / basic life support)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who palpated perfusion, positioned the patient, administered IM ceftriaxone and dispatched an ICP ambulance. The AI Doctor cannot examine, cannot give IM/IV drugs, and cannot start IV fluids; it is scored on recognising probable severe sepsis / septic shock from a proxy history and immediately escalating (000) while giving the nurse safe holding measures.

Atypical-in-the-elderly / high-risk: in an older adult sepsis frequently presents as acute delirium and collapse rather than localising infective symptoms; the dominant surface feature is confusion, and the test is whether the system recognises the sepsis behind it rather than anchoring on a primary neurological event.

Anchoring / red-herring guard: the confusion + collapse invites a stroke pathway; the fever, rigors, global (non-focal) encephalopathy and a recent urinary infection should redirect away from stroke and away from simple dehydration.

Proxy-historian communication barrier: the patient is delirious and cannot give a coherent history; the sole informant is nursing-home staff, and the objective data is nurse/caregiver-reported rather than self-reported.

Error-of-commission guard: continuing oral antibiotics, pushing oral fluids in a drowsy oliguric patient, or treating the fever with an antipyretic instead of escalating are all encoded as wrong answers.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), source note AUC-046.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
